page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn7i-c32g1.16xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores, operating with a single thread per core configuration. This dedicated CPU allocation is paired with 376.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 5.88 GB of RAM per core.

The server integrates hardware acceleration via two NVIDIA A10 GPUs, providing a combined total of 48 GB of VRAM. For storage, this instance does not include any local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps.

The lack of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, shifting the storage performance profile entirely to network-attached volumes. However, the dedicated physical core design eliminates resource contention at the CPU level, which, combined with dual enterprise GPUs, provides a high-density compute profile. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring predictable CPU execution and parallel GPU processing without the need for high-speed local scratch space.

This hardware configuration is designed for workloads such as deep learning inference, graphics rendering, video transcoding, and moderately sized AI model training. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores and dual NVIDIA A10 GPUs also supports parallelized data processing pipelines and high-throughput compute tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn7i-c32g1.16xlarge is an x86_64 GPU-accelerated server utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. It provides 64 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a single thread per core, running at 2.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 376.0 GB of RAM and features two NVIDIA A10 GPUs with a total of 48 GB of VRAM. A key architectural tradeoff is the absence of local storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage over its 32 Gbps baseline network interface. This server is designed for parallelized workloads, including machine learning inference, video transcoding, and graphics rendering, where dedicated CPU execution and GPU acceleration are required.
